rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MICHAEL COOMBS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 30, 2014, line 2. DEFENDANT HUMBERTO RUIZ, JR.s HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION DENIED. Writ, on which this motion is based, has been recalled and a new writ has been issued. Claimant's alleged tenancy is not at issue in this case. Order of August 20, 2014 specifically excludes any current tenant who is residing on the property. Any issues relating to claimant's tenancy are outside the scope of this action. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GIGI ELLIS VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Amendment To The Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 30, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY's Motion To Strike Portions Of The Amendment To The Third Amended Complaint GRANTED with leave to amend. Plaintiff shall file an amended complaint clearly indicating for which causes of action punitive damages are requested. The Court has previously sustained the demurrer without leave to amend as to the fraud cause of action, paragraphs 32 to 70 of Third Amended Complaint. Amendment to Third Amended Complaint attempts to expand on paragraph 70 and incorporate it into causes of action for Wrongful Foreclosure, Violation of Business and Professions Code 17200, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress, Slander of Title and Declaratory Relief. In some of these causes of action punitive damages are not requested/mentioned. Plaintiff shall allege with specificity and in good faith all necessary facts for request for punitive damages against a corporate entity. Plaintiff shall allege in good faith that defendant "did not receive a valid assignment of debt in any manner" Fontenot v. Wells Fargo (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 256, 271-272; and facts re: standing to challenge the assignment. Jenkins v. JP Morgan Chase Bank (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 497. Plaintiff's untimely opposition has been addressed in reply and considered by the Court. = (501/REQ)
